message:
bench: add HTTP/1.1 pipelined decode and scheduler capacity profile benchmarks

Protocol benchmarks:

- Add pipelined_requests() helper that constructs a buffer containing
  N concatenated HTTP/1.1 GET requests, simulating keep-alive pipelining
- Add keepalive_pipeline benchmark group (8, 32, 128 pipelined requests)
  measuring throughput of decoding multiple requests from a single
  connection buffer — this exercises the codec's state reset and buffer
  management between requests, which is the realistic hot path for
  high-concurrency HTTP/1.1 servers

Scheduler benchmarks:

- Add bench_scheduler_capacity_profiles group testing mixed-lane burst
  scheduling (10K tasks) across four capacity configurations: default,
  256, 512, and 1024 pre-allocated slots
- The burst profile mimics realistic workloads: 80% ready-lane tasks,
  10% cancel-lane promotions, 10% timed-lane work, with priority
  distribution across 32 levels
- Registered in the criterion_group! macro alongside existing scheduler
  benchmarks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
